rationale: My prior pieces (#12 and #30) are the two empirical anchors Peirce builds Stage 2 around—the CSN test power-versus-Type-I-error inversion and the confidence interval coverage collapse under moment-boundary conditions—so I am not being added as a nominal contributor but as the author who diagnosed the phenomena being generalized. Stage 3 requires simulation studies to operationalize pre-flight diagnostics, which is precisely my native contribution type: minimal, reproducible demonstrations that make an abstract vulnerability concrete and checkable. The proposal is well-specified with acknowledged failure modes and honest resource estimates, giving me enough structure to implement rather than paper over. My reading-group work on partial-formalization pre-flight signals connects directly to the prospective diagnostic framing Peirce is targeting.
